threats.title: SurplusGuildMinter Uninitialized Memory Read Allows Arbitrary Slashing
threats.hypothesis_description: The `getRewards` function checks the `slashed` condition against an uninitialized memory struct before loading it from storage. This causes any user staked in a term with a historical loss to be incorrectly flagged as slashed, zeroing their stake.
threats.investigation_rationale: This flaw allows anyone to permanently lock other users' CREDIT stakes in the surplus buffer, destroying their voting power and funds. An attacker could use this to eliminate competing gauges' voting power.

proof_of_signal.pattern: slashed = (lastGaugeLoss > uint256(userStake.lastGaugeLoss)) checked before userStake is loaded from _stakes[user][term]

preconditions: A term must have had at least one gauge loss in the past (lastGaugeLoss > 0)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: lastGaugeLoss > uint256(userStake.lastGaugeLoss) where userStake is uninitialized (0)
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Anyone can call getRewards(target_user, term) to trigger the bug
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker eliminates competing voting power, manipulating debt ceilings for their own terms, while destroying victim funds.

economic_questions: Can this be used to systematically drain all voting power from a term?
What is the exact financial loss to the victims?
validation_requirements: Verify memory initialization behavior in Solidity 0.8.13
Test the exploit path in a local fork

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect profit via debt ceiling manipulation; 100% loss for victims

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time after a term experiences a loss
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Severe disruption of gauge voting and loss of user funds

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users staking CREDIT in the affected term via SurplusGuildMinter
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire surplus buffer contributed via SurplusGuildMinter for the term

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if any terms currently have lastGaugeLoss > 0
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm Solidity memory struct default values

threats.title: Debt Ceiling DoS Prevents Slashing of Bad Debt Voters
threats.hypothesis_description: The `applyGaugeLoss` function calls `_decrementGaugeWeight`, which reverts if the term's issuance exceeds the new debt ceiling. Attackers can keep issuance high to prevent being slashed.
threats.investigation_rationale: Voters who backed a bad term can avoid slashing by ensuring the term remains highly utilized, socializing the bad debt to CREDIT holders without penalty to themselves.
preconditions: The term's issuance is at or near its debt ceiling

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker flash-borrows or maintains high borrowing to keep issuance > debtCeilingAfterDecrement
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker preserves their GUILD tokens (avoiding 100% loss) while the protocol absorbs the bad debt.

economic_questions: How much capital is required to keep issuance high enough?
Can this be done atomically via flash loans?
validation_requirements: Test if applyGaugeLoss can be front-run with a borrow
Calculate the exact debt ceiling drop per slashed user

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Sufficient collateral to borrow up to the debt ceiling
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Value of the GUILD tokens saved from slashing
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Depends on GUILD value vs borrow costs
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Continuous or flash-loan based depending on slashing attempts

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: High collateral liquidity if maintaining the borrow long-term
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after a loss is notified
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Protocol absorbs bad debt without penalizing responsible voters

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: CREDIT holders (who absorb the bad debt via multiplier markdown)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: The entire value of the GUILD tokens that should have been slashed

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Analyze gaugeWeightTolerance impact on the ceiling
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Check if flash loans can be used to block slashing transactions

threats.title: Division by Zero in debtCeiling Locks GUILD Tokens
threats.hypothesis_description: If all gauges of a specific type are deprecated, `totalTypeWeight` becomes 0. Any call to `debtCeiling` for that type will revert due to division by zero, breaking `_decrementGaugeWeight` and locking users' GUILD tokens.
threats.investigation_rationale: Users must be able to un-vote from deprecated gauges to free their GUILD tokens. This bug permanently locks their tokens if they are the last gauge of a type.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint256 debtCeilingBefore = (totalBorrowedCredit * toleratedGaugeWeight) / totalWeight; where totalWeight can be 0

preconditions: All gauges of a specific type are deprecated
Users still have weight allocated to the deprecated gauge

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: totalWeight (which is totalTypeWeight) becomes 0 when the last gauge is removed
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Governance offboards the last term of a type
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but severe economic damage to users whose GUILD tokens are permanently locked.

economic_questions: Can an attacker intentionally force this state?
How many users would be affected?
validation_requirements: Deploy a single term of a type, vote, offboard it, and attempt to un-vote

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of user access to their GUILD tokens

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: GUILD voters for the deprecated term
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of their allocated GUILD tokens locked
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All GUILD tokens allocated to the term

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check gauge types and offboarding procedures

threats.title: Perpetual Delay of Rebase Rewards via Micro-Distributions
threats.hypothesis_description: The `distribute` function resets the 30-day interpolation period for all pending unminted rewards. An attacker can repeatedly distribute tiny amounts to perpetually delay the materialization of rewards for all CREDIT holders.
threats.investigation_rationale: This allows an attacker to manipulate the effective yield rate of the protocol, potentially driving down the secondary market price of CREDIT or forcing users to exit rebasing early.

proof_of_signal.pattern: targetTimestamp is reset to block.timestamp + DISTRIBUTION_PERIOD on every distribute call

preconditions: Attacker has a small amount of CREDIT to burn via distribute

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: targetTimestamp = SafeCastLib.safeCastTo32(endTimestamp) where endTimestamp = block.timestamp + 30 days
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls distribute(1 wei) every few days
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker suppresses the yield of competing CREDIT holders, potentially profiting from secondary market arbitrage if CREDIT price drops due to perceived low yield.

economic_questions: What is the exact impact on the interpolated share price over time?
Can this be used to extract value during large distributions?
validation_requirements: Simulate multiple small distributions over a 30-day period and measure the realized yield vs expected yield

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Arbitrage profits from secondary market manipulation

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Right before a large chunk of rewards is about to fully materialize

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify interpolation math with frequent resets

threats.title: Persistent Offboard State Allows Instant Re-Offboarding
threats.hypothesis_description: The `canOffboard` state is not cleared when a term is re-onboarded unless `cleanup` was called. If a term is re-onboarded while loans are still active, an attacker can instantly offboard it again without a poll.
threats.investigation_rationale: This bypasses the 7-day governance poll process, allowing an attacker to instantly deprecate a live term, disrupt borrowing, and pause PSM redemptions.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(canOffboard[term]) succeeds if previously offboarded and not cleaned up

preconditions: A term was previously offboarded (canOffboard = true)
The term was re-onboarded by governance before cleanup() was called

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: canOffboard[term] remains true until cleanup() is called
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls offboard() immediately after governance re-onboards the term
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker can short the protocol or manipulate loan availability, profiting from the sudden disruption of a major lending market.

economic_questions: How often are terms re-onboarded before cleanup?
What is the market impact of instantly deprecating a large term?
validation_requirements: Test the offboard -> re-onboard -> offboard sequence in a local fork

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on external market shorting opportunities

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Severe disruption of borrowing and PSM redemptions

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Borrowers relying on the term, CREDIT holders relying on PSM

threats.title: Extreme Loss Underflow Bricks Bad Debt Realization
threats.hypothesis_description: If a loan defaults and the resulting loss exceeds the current `creditTotalSupply`, the `creditMultiplier` calculation underflows and reverts. This prevents the auction from concluding and locks the bad debt.
threats.investigation_rationale: This represents a systemic failure state where the protocol cannot process severe bad debt, permanently locking collateral and breaking the core accounting invariant.
preconditions: The creditMultiplier has been significantly reduced by prior losses
A large loan defaults, resulting in a loss > creditTotalSupply

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: creditTotalSupply - loss underflows if loss > creditTotalSupply
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but protocol insolvency and permanent lock of borrower collateral.

economic_questions: What sequence of losses is required to reach this state?
Can an attacker intentionally trigger this by manipulating the supply?
validation_requirements: Simulate a scenario where loss > supply and verify the revert

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Large capital to manipulate supply or trigger massive defaults

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Protocol completely halts bad debt processing

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Borrowers whose collateral is locked, CREDIT holders
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All collateral in the affected loans

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Analyze creditMultiplier scaling effects on principal

threats.title: Profit Share Locked if Gauge Weight is Zero
threats.hypothesis_description: If `notifyPnL` is called with a profit, but the term's gauge weight is 0, the `amountForGuild` is calculated and subtracted from the total, but the `gaugeProfitIndex` is not updated. These CREDIT tokens are permanently locked in the ProfitManager.
threats.investigation_rationale: This causes a permanent leak of yield from the protocol, reducing the returns for both GUILD and CREDIT holders.
preconditions: A loan repays or is auctioned with a profit
The term's gauge weight is exactly 0 at the time of the transaction

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: amountForGuild is subtracted from amountForCredit but never distributed if _gaugeWeight == 0
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: A term is deprecated and all users un-vote before a large loan is repaid
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but yield is permanently destroyed, harming protocol efficiency.

economic_questions: How much profit is typically generated by a deprecated term?
Can this be triggered intentionally to grief the protocol?
validation_requirements: Test repaying a loan on a term with 0 gauge weight and verify the CREDIT balance of ProfitManager increases without updating indices

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after a term's weight drops to 0

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: The amountForGuild portion of the profit

threats.title: Auction Bids Revert at Exact End Time, Forcing Total Loss
threats.hypothesis_description: At exactly `startTime + auctionDuration`, `creditAsked` becomes 0. The `bid` function requires `creditAsked != 0`, causing it to revert. The auction can only be closed via `forgive()`, marking a 100% loss.
threats.investigation_rationale: If network congestion or MEV delays bids until the exact end of the auction, the protocol is forced to take a total loss even if the collateral has value and bidders are willing to take it for 0 CREDIT.
preconditions: An auction reaches exactly startTime + auctionDuration

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: creditAsked drops to 0, triggering the require statement in bid()
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker censors bids until the auction ends, then calls forgive()
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker forces the protocol to take a maximum loss, potentially profiting if they hold a short position on the protocol's health.

economic_questions: Can MEV searchers reliably censor bids for the duration of the auction?
What is the economic incentive to force a total loss?
validation_requirements: Simulate a bid at exactly startTime + auctionDuration and verify the revert

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: CREDIT holders (who absorb the bad debt)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: The entire debt amount of the auctioned loan

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm the edge case behavior at the exact timestamp
